title: Review-enforced conventions have no structural forcing function as a class — all 12 conventions declare enforcement review or manual
description: Every convention in the conventions block declares enforcement "review" or "manual"; only FGAP-071 files the unenforced-ness of ONE convention (gap-arc-coherence). The class — a convention registry whose members are all honor-system while the framework's own doctrine treats manual enforcement as a gap (the filing-provenance and branch-discipline guards exist precisely because review-enforcement fails) — is unfiled. Surfaced as a coverage hole by the critical-focus outline derivation (dimension E).

evidence.reference: All shipped conventions carry enforcement review/manual; no invariant, hook, or gate consumes the conventions block's enforcement field.

impact: Convention compliance depends entirely on session discipline; violations (deviations this project has repeatedly experienced) surface only by later audit, never at act time.
proposed_resolution: Requires determination: per-convention triage of which are structurally enforceable (invariant / write-gate / build-gate per the filing-pipeline enforcement surface) vs genuinely review-only, with the enforcement field made consequential. Positioned with MILE-007.
